betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Uranium-233 & Radium-226§f

§aBred fissile material and a stable neutron source — essential for sustainable fuel cycles!§f

§6Uranium-233§f (atomic number 92):
• Unnatural uranium isotope acquired by §bbreeding thorium§f
• The fissile component of thorium fuel
• Can itself be bred further into U-235

§6Uses§f:
§b• Thorium Fuel§f — primary use, 1/3 of ThMEU composition
§b• RBMK HEU-233 Fuel§f — highly enriched, linear flux function (§edangerous!§f)
§b• PWR Fuel§f
§b• ZIRNOX Fuel§f
§b• Breeding U-235§f — U-233 + 300 flux → Np-237 in Breeding Reactor

§6Radium-226§f (atomic number 88):
• Mildly radioactive (§e7.5 RAD/s§f) decay element of uranium and plutonium

§6Production§f:
§b• Uranium/Thorium ore processing§f — primary source (byproduct)
§b• Cyclotron§f — Carbon box + Lead powder → Radium-226 powder

§6Uses§f:
§b• Ra226Be Neutron Sources§f — for RBMK, Research Reactor, and Chicago Pile
  • Passive flux function (constant 30 output)
  • Very safe, low heat (0.035 °C/flux)
  • Melting point only 700°C (easy to melt!)
§b• RTG Pellet§f — §e3 power/tick§f
  • Lifespan: §e11,520,000,000 ticks§f (~18 real years, 2,400 in-game years!)
  • Total lifetime power: §e34.56 GHE§f
  • Decays to: Lead
§b• Radium Coffee§f — joke consumable
§b• Planned§f: Glow-in-the-dark paint

§eTip§f: Start breeding Th-232 immediately once you have a breeding reactor online. The thorium cycle produces more U-233 than it consumes, making it a truly renewable fuel source for RBMK stabilization!
